mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects expanding consumer spending, which directly drives corporate earnings and pushes the S&P 500 higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns is below 0.10 over any three-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5%, the labor market reaches full employment, driving wage growth and increased consumer spending, which boosts corporate profits and lifts the S&P 500 over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In sessions where UNRATE is below 4.5%, the median 60-day forward return is not positive, or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad-based economic expansion with rising output and employment, compressing equity risk premiums and pushing the S&P 500 higher over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal from INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to achieve an information coefficient (IC) at least 0.02 higher than either series alone over a two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle defined by a rising federal funds rate, the central bank removes accommodation, increasing discount rates and pressuring equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 0.5% and rising for at least 3 months)
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, the mean 5-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1,300 thousand units, the housing market is robust, creating positive wealth effects and construction employment that supports consumer spending and drives the S&P 500 higher over the next month.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1300, the median 20-day forward return is zero or negative, or the positive hit rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with stagnant retail sales (RSAFS) points to stagflationary conditions where higher input costs erode margins while consumer demand stalls, pushing the S&P 500 lower over a quarter.
failure_condition: In months where both CPIAUCSL increases YoY and RSAFS declines MoM, the 60-day forward return is not negative in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a full employment regime (UNRATE below 5%), further declines in the unemployment rate amplify wage pressure and anticipated rate hikes, creating headwinds for equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: During full-employment regimes (UNRATE < 5%), a one-month decline in UNRATE is not followed by negative 20-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate rises above 1.0%, the market interprets further rate increases as excessively contractionary, raising the cost of capital and discounting future cash flows more heavily, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0%, the 60-day forward return is not negative in at least 55% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly less than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).
